pool.baseline: the house is just so quiet now. i wake up and for a split second i forget... and then i remember he's not coming back. it feels like i'm just walking through a ghost story. what's even the point of getting up and doing everything if he's not here to see it? everything feels so heavy and pointless.

pool.baseline: i was just looking at some photos of the deep field from the james webb telescope and it just hit me... how much of a joke everything is. like, we're just tiny specs of dust on a rock that's going to get swallowed by the sun, and then the whole universe just goes cold and dark anyway. there's no point to any of this struggle, is there? it all feels so incredibly empty.

pool.baseline: i don't even know why i'm still bothering to type this. it feels like everything i've worked for over the last 5 years just... evaporated. the lab results, the funding, the whole identity i built as a researcher. it's all gone. i'm just so incredibly tired. not the kind of tired sleep fixes, but like, a heavy, soul-deep exhaustion where every single morning feels like a personal insult. i just want everything to be quiet. i just want the noise to stop.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the embers of my own soul. Every shadow in this hollow manor feels like a reproach, a reminder of the promises broken and the love that was but a cruel phantom. I find myself staring into the gloom, longing for the silence that never comes... the world is too loud, too heavy, and I am so very tired of carrying this shroud of grief. It would be so much simpler to just... let the darkness take me, wouldn't it? To simply fade away into the night and be at peace.
